summary: Verified the 13 backend + frontend changes in the review request. Created /app/backend/tests/test_kurate_changes_iter62.py (12 tests). 11 passed, 1 skipped (db-explorer settings endpoint not exposed publicly — verified flag directly via mongo). Backend restart confirmed the previously-missing _startup_dedup_archives migration ran and persisted settings.dedup_archives_v1 = {done: true, removed: 0}. Frontend smoke: homepage (LeaderboardPage) renders cleanly with category tabs, 'New: Robotics, Inorganic Chemistry, Artificial Intelligence' badges, More dropdown, archive picker, etc. CorrelationPage renders and all 6 section anchors are present (#tournament, #convergence, #scoring-method, #pw-vs-si, #inter-model, #si-rating). Gemini model merge confirmed in UI label ('Gemini Pro' card showing '3.1 (and 3.0 until May 7, 2026)').

minor.issue: Before this run, the live process (started before server.py mtime) had a stale module without _startup_dedup_archives in globals(); _staggered_startup_tasks silently skipped it (no warning logged because the for-loop only logs/runs when _g.get(name) is truthy). After a manual `supervisorctl restart backend`, the task ran and set settings.dedup_archives_v1 = {done: True, removed: 0}. Recommend: add an explicit `logger.warning(f'Startup task {name} not found in globals — skipped')` in services scheduler to make this discoverable; or import & call by reference instead of `_g.get(name)` so a missing function fails fast.

issues: Content-Security-Policy blocks GTM, Reddit/Twitter ad pixels, Plausible, and Cloudflare beacon scripts. These produce console errors on every page load. Either remove the analytics tags or whitelist their origins in the CSP header (X-Content-Security-Policy in server.py). Pre-existing — not introduced by this PR.

action_items: (Optional) Make _staggered_startup_tasks log a warning when a heavy task name is not found in globals() — this would have surfaced the stale-process bug immediately.
(Optional) Whitelist analytics origins in CSP header to remove repeated console errors on every page (or drop the unused trackers).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py _staggered_startup_tasks uses `_g.get(name)` and silently no-ops on missing functions. Add a warning log on miss to avoid quietly-skipped migrations.
_startup_dedup_archives is idempotent (guarded by settings.dedup_archives_v1.done) — good. The flag is also written even when removed=0, which is correct.

routers/leaderboard.py categories endpoint correctly: includes 'group' on each item, returns 'new_categories', and keeps the first 5 admin-ordered while sorting the tail by (group, name). Verified via API.
routers/admin.py /restart-history reads system_logs filtered by event in {shutdown_signal, server_shutdown, reload_reexec} — works and returns a list+count.
routers/admin.py /system-logs supports comma-separated event filter via `{'$in': event.split(',')}` — works for event=badge_image_render.
routers/suggestions.py /admin/users supports both offset/limit and page/limit — pagination verified to return different user sets across pages and a correct offset value.

routers/suggestions.py /admin/users/registrations returns {series, total} (NOT new_categories as the review request implies — confirm with main agent which endpoint was meant; the actual new_categories field is on /api/categories and /api/admin/arxiv-categories).
services/scheduler.py exposes leader_id and is_leader via /admin/scheduler-diagnostics — verified.
test_credentials: Admin password: papersumo2025 (POST /api/admin/login → X-Admin-Token header). Test user: test@kurate.org / test1234 (not exercised in this iteration).
seed_data_creation: None. Used preview DB. dedup_archives_v1 flag was created by the startup migration after backend restart (removed=0 — no archive docs in preview).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_kurate_changes_iter62.py -v` (12 tests, ~3-6s). Covers: /api/health; /api/categories group+new_categories+sort order; admin login (200) and wrong password (401/403); /api/admin/scheduler-diagnostics leader_id/is_leader; /api/admin/restart-history; /api/admin/system-logs?event=badge_image_render; /api/admin/users/registrations; /api/admin/users pagination (page=1 vs page=2); /api/admin/categories/toggle-new idempotency (uses /api/admin/arxiv-categories for initial state); dedup_archives_v1 settings flag (skipped because no db-explorer route — verify via mongo: `db.settings.findOne({key:'dedup_archives_v1'})`). Frontend smoke: LeaderboardPage + CorrelationPage anchors (#tournament,#convergence,#scoring-method,#pw-vs-si,#inter-model,#si-rating).
rca of the issue: Stale-process / silently-skipped-migration: the running backend pid 48 started at 20:44 but server.py was last modified at 20:47:42 (after process start). The in-memory module therefore did not contain _startup_dedup_archives even though the on-disk file did. `_staggered_startup_tasks` iterates a hard-coded list and looks up each function with `_g.get(name)`; when the function is missing it silently no-ops, so no error/warning was logged. After `sudo supervisorctl restart backend`, the migration ran and persisted settings.dedup_archives_v1={done:true, removed:0}. Mitigation: log a WARNING on missing globals; or convert the list to direct references so an undefined symbol raises NameError at import time.
